name: ai-sharepoint
description: Govern and explore SharePoint Online with GitHub Copilot — enterprise auth, read-only reference sources, and privacy-first diagnostics.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Authentication settings (authority, client ID, authority allowlist) are machine-scoped and cannot be overridden by workspace settings in untrusted workspaces.

commands.title: Sign Out of Site (wipe cached credentials)
Build Database ER Diagram (probe join rates)…

viewsWelcome.contents:
No SharePoint sites connected yet.
[Connect a Site](command:aiSharePoint.connectSite)
Sign in with your Microsoft account — browser or device-code. Commercial, GCC High (.us) and 21Vianet (.cn) clouds are supported.
Credentials are stored only in your OS keychain. [Privacy & data notice](command:aiSharePoint.openPrivacyNotice)

GitHub Copilot Chat is not installed. AI SharePoint runs all AI features through your own Copilot entitlement — no extra keys.
[Install GitHub Copilot Chat](command:aiSharePoint.installCopilotChat)
After installing, sign in to GitHub when prompted.

Copilot Chat is installed, but no Copilot models are available yet — sign in to GitHub (Accounts menu, bottom-left) with an account that has a Copilot subscription or seat.
[Check Copilot Status](command:aiSharePoint.checkCopilotStatus)

No reference sources yet. Connect read-only context the assistant can search — Confluence and Jira (Cloud or Data Center) to start; more source types are on the roadmap.
[Add Context Source](command:aiSharePoint.addContextSource)
Credentials stay in your OS keychain; every source is strictly read-only with account-lockout protection.

No drafts pending. Prepare a Teams chat or Outlook email — yourself or by asking @sharepoint to draft one — and it waits here until YOU approve sending it.
[Draft Teams Message](command:aiSharePoint.draftTeamsMessage)
[Draft Outlook Email](command:aiSharePoint.draftOutlookEmail)
Nothing is ever sent without your per-message approval (ADR-0025).

Projects group reference sources & bookmarks, define **goals** and **instructions** for @sharepoint, and keep a separate **AI-managed context** the assistant learns over time.
[Create Project](command:aiSharePoint.createProject)
Activate a project to scope chat and the Reference Sources view to it.

chatParticipants.name: sharepoint
chatParticipants.description: Ask about your SharePoint sites, plans, and Copilot usage

commands.name: site
commands.description: Live overview of a connected site (lists, pages)

commands.name: usage
commands.description: This extension's Copilot request activity (local counts)

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_list_connections
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List the user's configured SharePoint site connections (name, URL, role, verified). Read-only. Use it to discover which sites are available before other SharePoint tools.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_site_overview
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get a live, read-only overview of a connected SharePoint site: title, description, lists/libraries, and modern pages. Pass the site URL or display name; omit it if only one connection exists.

site.description: Site URL or display name of a configured connection. Optional when exactly one connection exists.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_list_pages
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List the modern pages of a connected SharePoint site (title, URL, last modified). Read-only. Pass the site URL or display name; omit it if only one connection exists.

site.description: Site URL or display name of a configured connection. Optional when exactly one connection exists.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_copilot_usage
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Report the requests this extension made through the user's Copilot subscription (factual local counts: today/this month, failures, by model with tokens). Premium-request consumption against the user's plan is NOT tracked — the GitHub billing page is the authoritative source.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_list_sources
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List the user's configured read-only reference sources (Confluence, Jira, LDAP/Active Directory, SQL Server/PostgreSQL/MySQL/MongoDB databases) with their chat alias (the short handle users say, e.g. "CMDB"), description of contents, type, deployment, and verification state. Use before searching to discover what is available and map a name the user mentioned to a source.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_search_context
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search a configured read-only reference source. Confluence: raw CQL or plain text. Jira: raw JQL or plain text. LDAP/Active Directory: raw LDAP filter or plain text (AD ANR). Databases (SQL Server/PostgreSQL/MySQL): a single read-only SELECT statement (write keywords are blocked). MongoDB: JSON {"collection","filter","projection","limit"}. Returns capped, cached results. Pass the source name when several are configured. If a query proves useful for recurring work, propose saving it with the suggest-bookmark tool (user-approved). Vertex AI Search sources: plain-text queries return enterprise search hits (use the vertex_answer tool for synthesized answers). Power BI sources: JSON {"dataset": "<id or name>", "dax": "EVALUATE …"} runs a read-only DAX query (use db_schema-style starter EVALUATE INFO.TABLES() to discover tables). ServiceNow sources: plain text (text-index search of the default table), a native encoded query ("active=true^priority=1"), or JSON {"table": "cmdb_ci_appl", "query": "...", "fields": [...], "limit": n}. Splunk sources: plain keywords (default index, last 24h), raw SPL ("search index=web error | stats count by host", "| tstats ..."), or JSON {"spl": "...", "earliest": "-7d", "latest": "now", "limit": n} — write/exfil commands (delete/collect/outputlookup/sendemail/script) are blocked.

source.description: Source alias (e.g. "CMDB"), display name, or type — as the user said it. Optional when exactly one source exists.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_get_context_item
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetch one item from a reference source with plain-text body: a Confluence page by id, a Jira issue by key (e.g. ENG-42), or an LDAP/Active Directory entry by distinguished name (DN). Databases have no item fetch — use search with a SELECT statement instead. Read-only. ServiceNow: fetch one record as "table/sys_id".

source.description: Source alias (e.g. "CMDB"), display name, or type — as the user said it. Optional when exactly one source exists.

id.description: Confluence page id, Jira issue key, or LDAP distinguished name (DN).

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_list_bookmarks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List the user's saved reference bookmarks (named, reusable queries or item locators) with their source and kind. Use before running a bookmark by name.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_run_bookmark
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a saved reference bookmark by name: executes its stored query (search) or fetches its stored item against the bookmark's source. Read-only, cached, capped.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_suggest_bookmark
languageModelTools.modelDescription: After searching the reference sources (Confluence, Jira, LDAP/AD) and identifying a query or item the user is likely to reuse — e.g. a Jira queue/JQL for their initiative, a Confluence space query, a person's directory entry — propose persisting it as a named bookmark. The user approves via a confirmation dialog before anything is saved; nothing persists without approval. Provide a short, descriptive name, the exact locator (raw CQL/JQL/LDAP filter for kind=query; page id/issue key/DN for kind=item), and a one-line reason.

source.description: Source alias (e.g. "CMDB"), display name, or type — as the user said it. Optional when exactly one source exists.

locator.description: CQL/JQL/LDAP filter (query) or page id / issue key / DN (item).

reason.description: One line on why this is worth bookmarking (shown to the user).

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_db_schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Look up a database source's schema before writing SQL for a free-form question. Returns tables and columns (names + types), and — when the schema has been semantically indexed — concept tags and synonyms per column (e.g. a column group_cio tagged 'ownership', aka 'owner/CIO'), so questions like 'records owned by X' map to the right columns. Pass a topic ('ownership', 'application servers', the user's phrasing) to get only matching tables; omit it to list tables. The catalog is loaded automatically on first use (metadata only). Then run your SELECT with the search tool.

source.description: Database source alias (e.g. "CMDB"), display name, or type. Optional when exactly one source exists.

topic.description: Concept to find columns for — e.g. 'ownership', 'owned by', 'cost', 'application'. Omit to list tables.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_vertex_answer
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ask a configured Vertex AI Search source (Google enterprise search) for a Gemini-grounded ANSWER with citations over its indexed corpus — use for analytical questions ('summarize our policy on X') where synthesized analysis beats raw hits. For raw result lists use the regular search tool against the same source. Read-only.

source.description: Vertex AI Search source alias, display name, or type. Optional when exactly one source exists.

query.description: The question to answer over the enterprise corpus.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_draft_communication
languageModelTools.modelDescription: PREPARE (never send) a Microsoft Teams chat message or an Outlook email to specific individuals, e.g. after research the user asked to share. The draft goes to the extension's Communications outbox where the USER must review and explicitly approve it before anything is sent — you cannot send, and must tell the user the draft awaits their approval in the Communications view. Provide recipients as emails/UPNs (max 10), a subject for email, and a plain-text body.

subject.description: Email subject (required for outlook, ignored for teams).

reason.description: One line on why this communication is being prepared (shown to the user).

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_index_db_schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Build (or rebuild) the semantic index of a database source's schema using the user's Copilot entitlement: table/column NAMES ONLY are sent (never data rows), batched and metered. The user approves in a confirmation dialog first. Offer this when db_schema reports no semantic index and the user asks free-form questions about the database's records — afterwards concepts like ownership ('owned by X') resolve to the right columns (e.g. group_cio).

source.description: Database source alias, display name, or type. Optional when exactly one source exists.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_write_site_files
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Write lists/<name>.json and pages/<name>.json spec files into a managed site's local repository (the same format pull_site produces — mirror pulled files for structure). Local only: nothing reaches SharePoint until apply_site is approved. Use this to IMPLEMENT a site design you produced, then call apply_site. Requires the site to have a configured repository.

site.description: Managed site URL or display name. Optional when exactly one managed site exists.

reason.description: One line on what this implements (shown to the user).

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_pull_site
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Launch the preview-first pull of a managed site's live state into its local repository (no SharePoint writes). Use before writing spec files when the repo may be stale, and to learn the exact file format. The user confirms the preview.

site.description: Managed site URL or display name. Optional when exactly one managed site exists.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_apply_site
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Launch the guarded write-back of a managed site's repository to SharePoint: the user sees the operation-level preview, deletions are opt-in, a safety snapshot is taken, and NOTHING changes until they approve the dialog. Call after write_site_files. Never state the site changed until the user's approval completed — verify with site_overview afterwards.

site.description: Managed site URL or display name. Optional when exactly one managed site exists.

languageModelTools.name: aisharepoint_remember_project_context
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Persist a durable, project-specific learning into the ACTIVE project's AI-managed context (separate from the user's own goals/instructions) so it carries across sessions — e.g. 'the user wants app-ownership answers from the CMDB, citing sys_id', 'always answer in German'. The user approves each note in a confirmation dialog. Use sparingly for genuinely durable behavior the user taught you, not per-turn details. No-op if no project is active.

note.description: One concise learning to remember for this project.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with AI SharePoint
walkthroughs.description: Connect a site, chat with @sharepoint, stay in control of Copilot spend, and know exactly what leaves your machine (nothing, unless you export it).

steps.title: Install GitHub Copilot Chat
steps.description:
AI features run through your own Copilot entitlement via the VS Code Language Model API — provided by the **GitHub Copilot Chat** extension (marketplace search: “github copilot chat”). No extra keys, nothing routed through third parties.
This step completes automatically when Copilot Chat is installed and you are signed in.
[Install GitHub Copilot Chat](command:aiSharePoint.installCopilotChat)
[Check Copilot Status](command:aiSharePoint.checkCopilotStatus)

media.altText: Copilot powers AI SharePoint through your own entitlement

steps.title: Connect a SharePoint site
steps.description:
Sign in with your Microsoft account (browser or device-code) and connect a site as **managed** or read-only **reference**. Credentials are cached only in your OS keychain.
[Connect a Site](command:aiSharePoint.connectSite)

steps.title: Chat with @sharepoint
steps.description:
Ask **@sharepoint** about your sites in the Chat view — try `/site` for a live overview. In agent mode, Copilot can call the read-only tools itself: site overviews, pages, reference sources (Confluence/Jira/LDAP), bookmarks, and usage.
[Open Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%22%40sharepoint%20%2Fhelp%22)

steps.title: See what the extension asked Copilot
steps.description:
The Copilot Activity view and dashboard show factual, locally measured request counts (today, this month, by model and task). Premium-request consumption against your plan is **not** estimated — your GitHub billing page is the authoritative source.
[Open Activity Dashboard](command:aiSharePoint.showUsage)

steps.title: Diagnostics that respect your enterprise
steps.description:
Usage counters and redacted error reports are stored **locally only**. When you need support, export an **anonymized bundle** — previewed and leak-scanned before a single byte is written.
[Export Diagnostics Bundle](command:aiSharePoint.exportDiagnostics)
[Privacy & Data Notice](command:aiSharePoint.openPrivacyNotice)

aiSharePoint.copilot.preferredModelFamil.markdownDescription: Model family this extension uses by default (set via **AI SharePoint: List Copilot Models**). Empty = cheapest entitled model (economy-first policy).

aiSharePoint.auth.tenantAuthority.markdownDescription: Microsoft Entra ID authority for sign-in (MSAL public client). Machine-scoped: workspaces cannot override it. The host must be a known Microsoft login endpoint or listed in `#aiSharePoint.auth.additionalAuthorityHosts#`.

aiSharePoint.auth.clientId.markdownDescription: Optional custom Entra application (client) ID for tenants that block the default Microsoft Graph PowerShell first-party app. Requires a public-client app registration with `http://localhost` redirect and delegated `Sites.Read.All` — see the Admin Guide.

aiSharePoint.auth.additionalAuthorityHos.markdownDescription: Extra allowed sign-in authority hosts (beyond the built-in Microsoft login endpoints). Machine-scoped.

enumDescriptions: Capture local usage counters only while VS Code telemetry is enabled (defers to your org's telemetry stance).
aiSharePoint.diagnostics.usageCapture.markdownDescription: Local feature-usage counters for the diagnostics bundle. **Nothing is ever transmitted by the extension** — data leaves the machine only via your explicit export.

aiSharePoint.diagnostics.errorCapture.markdownDescription: Store redacted error reports locally for the diagnostics bundle. Disable to keep no error history at all.

aiSharePoint.sync.allowedRemoteHosts.markdownDescription: Git hosts that site repositories may push to (ADR-0019). Add your GitHub Enterprise Server host, e.g. `github.corp.example`. Machine-scoped: workspaces cannot override it.

aiSharePoint.context.cacheTtlMinutes.markdownDescription: How long reference-source results are cached locally (ADR-0011). 0 disables caching.

aiSharePoint.context.maxResults.markdownDescription: Maximum results per reference-source query (ADR-0012 read-safety cap).

aiSharePoint.context.catalogTtlHours.markdownDescription: How long a pre-cached source catalog (Confluence spaces / Jira projects+queues) stays fresh. After expiry the next browse offers a refresh; the expired copy remains usable.

aiSharePoint.context.catalogCheckpointSe.markdownDescription: While pre-caching a large catalog, ask every N seconds whether to continue. Loading pauses while the prompt waits, so the source system is never overtaxed.

aiSharePoint.context.allowSchemaIndexing.markdownDescription: Allow building **semantic schema indexes** of database sources with Copilot (ADR-0024). Only table/column **names and types** are ever sent — never row data — and always behind an explicit per-source consent. Set `false` to disable organization-wide.

aiSharePoint.ldap.tlsRejectUnauthorized.markdownDescription: Validate LDAPS/StartTLS server certificates for LDAP/Active Directory sources (ADR-0020). Keep **true** in production. If LDAPS uses an internal CA not in Node's bundle, prefer installing the CA; only disable for isolated lab testing.

aiSharePoint.ldap.useStartTls.markdownDescription: For `ldap://` (port 389) sources, upgrade the connection with StartTLS before binding. Ignored for `ldaps://` (implicit TLS).

aiSharePoint.ldap.caCertificatesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a PEM CA bundle trusted for LDAPS/StartTLS connections (e.g. your corporate root + intermediates), **appended** to Node's defaults and the OS trust store. Machine-scoped. Use when the OS-store API is unavailable on your VS Code runtime — see Admin Guide §7.

aiSharePoint.logging.verboseWire.markdownDescription: Log the **full request/response detail of every integration** — Microsoft Graph (SharePoint, Teams, Outlook), Confluence/Jira, LDAP/Active Directory, SQL Server/PostgreSQL/MySQL/MongoDB, Vertex AI Search, Power BI, MSAL sign-in traffic, and Copilot prompts/responses — to the *AI SharePoint* output channel. **Secrets are redacted in layers**: authorization headers are reduced to their scheme, token-endpoint bodies are withheld entirely, credential-shaped fields are masked, and every line passes the global redaction filter. Database/LDAP **result data is summarized (counts/columns), not dumped**. Local only; never included in diagnostics exports. Expect large output — enable only while debugging.

aiSharePoint.ldap.dnsServers.markdownDescription: **Machine-scoped.** Internal DNS server IPs used for Active Directory **SRV lookups** (e.g. `["10.0.0.53", "10.0.1.53"]`). Set this when LDAP works in the office but fails on VPN: Node's resolver bypasses Windows NRPT / VPN split-DNS rules, so pointing SRV queries directly at corporate DNS by IP makes discovery deterministic everywhere. Empty = system resolver.

aiSharePoint.servicenow.oauthClientId.markdownDescription: **Machine-scoped.** OAuth client ID for **ServiceNow browser sign-in** — from a one-time Application Registry entry (System OAuth → *Create an OAuth API endpoint for external clients*) with redirect URL `http://localhost:51725/callback`. Users then sign in via their browser SSO session; no passwords or hand-issued tokens. Leave empty to hide the browser option.
